summary: Route IM (D-prefixed) channel ids and a new --user flag through conversations.open before reading conversations.history.
reason: conversations history --channel <D...> failed with channel_not_found even when the IM id had just been listed by conversations list --types im and a valid SLACK_USER_TOKEN was set. Raw IM ids from conversations.list are not directly accepted by conversations.history unless the DM is opened/resolved first with the user token. The fix detects D-prefixed channel ids and routes them through the DM helpers (conversations.open + user-token history fetch), adds a --user flag that resolves the DM from a user id, auto-retries channel_not_found by re-opening the DM from its peer, and improves the error message to point users to --user. It also activates the previously dead slack_dm_helpers.go helpers. The DM path returns the bare messages array (slackEnvelopeMessages) so its output matches the channel path, and --channel/--user are rejected as mutually exclusive.
validated_outcome: go build, go vet, and go test pass; conversations.open is called via POST (Slack documents it as a write method; GET would fail at runtime); --channel <D...> and --user resolve the DM via conversations.open instead of returning channel_not_found; DM output renders as a messages array (provenance count and table no longer silently empty); --channel + --user errors as mutually exclusive.
